description_long: This object consists of three spherical clusters of vibrant purple petals, each forming a dense, rounded inflorescence. The petals are arranged in a whorled, overlapping pattern, giving the flower heads a textured, almost geometric appearance. The flower heads are supported by slender, green stems that branch out from a central point, with each stem holding a single flower head. The stems are smooth and cylindrical, with no visible leaves attached. The overall structure is upright and delicate, resembling ornamental allium or globe amaranth flowers commonly found in urban parks and landscaped gardens. The vivid purple color of the petals contrasts sharply with the bright green stems, making the object visually striking.
description: A cluster of three spherical, purple-petaled flower heads on green stems, with a dense, whorled petal arrangement and no visible leaves.
description_view_0: Two purple flower heads are visible, one upright and one angled, both with dense, whorled petals and green stems.
description_view_1: All three purple flower heads are visible, showing their spherical shape and the branching of the green stems.
description_view_2: The three flower heads are seen from the front, highlighting their round, dense petal clusters and the central stem.
description_view_3: Two flower heads are prominent, with one partially obscured, and the green stems are clearly visible branching from the base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, whorled, and overlapping radial pattern, forming nearly spherical flower heads with clear symmetry and layering.
